pi_items.source: task.json specification; validated against Azure DevOps feed naming conventions
Azure Artifacts documentation confirms retention policies support configurable maximum versions per package; 15 is well below the 5,000 hard limit per package ID
Azure Artifacts official Microsoft Learn documentation confirms both NuGet Gallery and npmjs are supported as predefined upstream sources in the Artifacts feed creation UI
environment setup script (setup_azure_devops.ps1) creates project 'TailwindTraders' at Phase 3
setup_azure_devops.ps1 (lines 23-26) and task_utils.ps1 (lines 16-18) both use 'http://localhost/DefaultCollection' as the primary candidate URL for Azure DevOps Server

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task running on Azure DevOps Server (local Docker environment). The task requires creating an Artifact Feed named 'Tailwind-Universal' in the TailwindTraders project (at http://localhost/DefaultCollection) with exactly two upstream sources: NuGet Gallery and npmjs. The feed must have a retention policy enforcing a maximum of 15 versions per package. The verifier script (verifier.py) checks feed existence (30 pts), retention limit value of exactly 15 (30 pts), and presence of both NuGet and npm upstream sources (40 pts split), requiring a minimum score of 70 to pass.
